How much should you tip for tattoo removal?

At dedicated tattoo removal studios, tip 15–20% of the session cost. For a $200 session, that's $30–40. At medical spas with licensed medical staff, tipping is appreciated but less strictly expected, and 10–15% is a fair gesture if you want to tip.

The tipping norm depends largely on the setting. Dedicated removal studios follow tipping customs similar to tattoo shops. Medical spas and dermatology offices operate more like healthcare facilities where tipping is unusual.

Tattoo removal technicians are skilled laser operators who adjust settings for ink type, skin tone, and treatment area. Each session requires careful technique and aftercare guidance.

Factors that affect how much to tip

  • Setting type: Dedicated studios expect 15–20%. Medical spas 10–15% optional. Dermatology offices and clinics typically don't expect tips at all.
  • Session complexity: Large tattoos, stubborn inks, or heavily layered areas require more passes and more careful technique. Tip toward the 20% end for complex sessions.
  • Multi-session commitment: Tattoo removal typically requires 6–12 sessions. Tip at each individual session, as each is a complete service.
  • Aftercare guidance: Technicians who provide thorough aftercare instructions and check-ins between sessions deserve recognition at the higher end of the range.
  • If you purchased a package, tip per session based on the package session rate, not a prorated amount.

Tattoo removal tipping etiquette

Tip at each individual session, not just the first or last. Each visit is a complete service with real technique and aftercare guidance.

Don't reduce your tip for slow fading. Results depend heavily on ink type, skin, and immune response, factors outside the technician's control. Tip based on service quality, not outcome pace.

Cash is always appreciated but card tipping is more common at studios and med spas than at traditional tattoo shops. Either method is fine.
